title: Kushiban
content:

'''Kushibans''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] lagomorphs, with two big ears and [[mood fur]] covering their bodies. This mood fur was white when the Kushiban were at peace, and changed colors according to their mood.

==Biology and appearance==
Members of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] alien species known as Kushibans were four-limbed lagomorphs,<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /><ref name="NJ">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref><ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Their bodies were commonly half a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] long,<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /><ref name="NJ" /> but could reach one meter, as exemplified by Kushiban Jedi Master [[Ikrit]].<ref name="J1" /><ref name="J4" /><ref name="J6">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Kenobi's Blade]]''</ref> Like many smaller species, they were physically weaker than most humanoids, but they were also a difficult target due to their small size.<ref name="UA" /> They were also nimble, quick and stealthy when needed.<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="NJ" /><ref name="UA" />

The head of a Kushiban was dominated by the two big,<ref name="J1" /><ref name="J4" /><ref name="J6" /> soft<ref name="J1" /><ref name="J4" /><ref name="J6" /><ref name="UA" /><ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> ears, which a Kushiban could rise or drop at will or to reflect changes of mood: When disappointed<ref name="J4" /> or tired a Kushiban dropped the ears,<ref name="EV3">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' Chapter 3</ref> but they would revive if the Kushiban recovered interest in a subject<ref name="J4" /> or to focus his or her attention. The ears rocked when a Kushiban nodded,<ref name="J5">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Vader's Fortress]]''</ref> and fell to the floor when a Kushiban was sleeping.<ref name="J1" />

The face of a Kushiban, which had apelike features,<ref name="EC" /> included a keen<ref name="UA" /> small<ref name="PJ" /> snout,<ref name="UA" /> but was dominated by its big,<ref name="J1" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /><ref name="EC" /><ref name="J6" /><ref name="J3">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Promises]]''</ref> innocent-looking,<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> round eyes,<ref name="J3" /> with eyelids protruded for several centimeter. Kushibans were known to wink eyes<ref name="J1" /> and to watch people closely while silently waiting for the other person to talk.<ref name="J3" /> The eye color, in the case of Jedi Master Ikrit, could change to mimic the eye color of the person the Kushiban was looking at or becoming a whirlpool if the Kushiban was not looking at anyone at the moment.<ref name="J1" /> Ikrit's eyes were known to be bright, except during the end of his life,<ref name="EV3" /> and Human Jedi [[Tionne Solusar]] recognized in them curiosity and intelligence.<ref name="J4" />

The body of a Kushiban, including its soft<ref name="J6" /> tail,<ref name="J4" /> was covered with surprisingly<ref name="J1" /> soft<ref name="J1" /><ref name="UA" /> but straight-out,<ref name="J1" /> fluffy<ref name="J5" /> fur.<ref name="EC" /> While the Kushiban was at rest, its fur was frost white,<ref name="J1" /><ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> but that feature was not fixed:<ref name="EC" /> the Kushiban could change the color of its fur at will<ref name="J6" /> emulating for instance the golden-brown of surrounding stones<ref name="J1" /><ref name="J3" /> to camouflage itself for security<ref name="J4" /> or reflecting the Kushiban's mood,<ref name="J4" /><ref name="UA" /><ref name="NJ" /> blossoming to pitch black when mourning,<ref name="J4" /> or desperate<ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> and changing to grey when upset.<ref name="J6" /> Due to this, a Kushiban's fur was known as "[[mood fur]]."<ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> The few people familiar with the Kushibans changing colors depending on their mood<ref name="J4" /> could use this knowledge to understand the real motivations of a Kushiban.<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" />

Kushiban fur could be, and was, curled, and individual Kushibans tended to brush their fur daily. If a Kushiban got its fur dirty, he or she could shake it off to get rid of dust or mud; otherwise, the fur tended to get filthy and matted.<ref name="J4" />

While Kushibans were quadrupeds, their forepaws could be used as manipulative hands,<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> allow a Kushiban to open a door, to hold an object,<ref name="J5" /> or to beat its chest while standing on its hind legs.<ref name="J1" /> Indeed, Kushibans could walk on two legs, but they were faster when running or ramming on all fours,<ref name="J1" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> and tended to walk on all fours as default choice.<ref name="EV3" /> A Kushiban could also leap, reaching the shoulder of an eleven-year-old human, the upper dome of an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2 unit]],<ref name="J1" /> or the frame of a window,<ref name="J4" /> and they could also climb up steep walls<ref name="J2">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Lyric's World]]''</ref> or up the body of a friendly humanoid.<ref name="J4" />

Kushibans could speak either [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] or other languages. In the case of Kushiban Jedi Master Ikrit, his voice when speaking Basic was raspy,<ref name="J2" /> deep<ref name="J3" /> and scratchy,<ref name="J1" /><ref name="J5" /><ref name="J6" /><ref name="EV3" /> with a familiar rasp.<ref name="EV4">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' Chapter 4</ref> At other times, it was between a purr, a growl and a roar, and he had a peculiar wheezing sound when laughing.<ref name="J4" />

A Kushiban started adolescence at six years of age, and was considered an adult at thirteen. When reaching sixty years, the Kushiban felt the first ill effects of old age, which became more acute at eighty-five. A Kushiban's life expectancy reached one hundred and ten years of age,<ref name="UA" /> although a number of Kushibans outlived that span.<ref name="J1" />

Kushibans were herbivorous creatures and ate all their food raw,<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> but were known to bite while attacking.<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="NJ" /><ref name="UA" />

A number of Kushibans were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and became members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], as in the case of Ikrit. An exceptional Kushiban, Ikrit was a [[Telepathy/Legends|telepath]] who could hibernate for four centuries and communicate with an R2 unit without nearby Humans hearing him.<ref name="J1" />

Due to their furry appearance and a number of their behavioral patterns, Kushibans were considered cute by a number of other species. Eleven-year old Human [[Tahiri Veila]] found Ikrit to be cute before knowing that he was a sentient being.<ref name="J1" /> A number of people described the Kushiban [[K'Mi]] as cute, which K'Mi found aggravating.<ref name="AM">{{LivingForce|A Mon Alone}}</ref>

==Society and culture==
[[File:Ikrit-PotJSB.jpg|thumb|right|170px|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ikrit]] was a Kushiban.]]
The Kushiban people considered themselves to be a simple civilization,<ref name="J4" /> having developed a complex and peaceful society<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="NJ" /><ref name="UA" /> that lived harmoniously with their environment<ref name="PJ" /> on the non-industrialized, lush<ref name="UA" /> green<ref name="EA" /> planet [[Kushibah]], in the [[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]]<ref name="EAO">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="J4" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="NJ" /><ref name="UA" />

The Kushibans shared their planet with non-sentient, clawed, fanged, three-meter predators known as [[Xinkra]]s<ref name="J4" /> or Xinkra-beasts.<ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /> The Xinkras preyed on the Kushibans, but the Kushibans had learned to use fire as a defense against<ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /> all their natural predators.<ref name="UA" /> The pyrophobic Xinkras were also vulnerable to sharp weapons and to Jedi mind tricks.<ref name="J4" />

Kushibans lived in family units, similar to Human's, in collective areas of weavers and farmers: Kushiban farmers grew [[silkweed]] which, when mixed with their own brushed fur, could be weaved to create clothes and tapestry. Kushiban fabric was famous throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], leading to a local industry and a number of traditions: The Silkweed harvest was a notable date in Kushibah. Due to the textile jobs, Kushibans used a number of specific tools including rakes, scythes, spindles and torches,<ref name="J4" /> but beyond that the Kushiban population held little interest for galactic technology.<ref name="EA" /> Kushibans had little need for tools, as they needed not to hunt or cook their food and used caves and hollow trees to protect themselves from weather.<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> As Kushibah had so little technology,<ref name="EA" /> the world was almost worthless for the space-based economy of the galactic community.<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" />

Kushibans were commonly kind and generous, with a playful sense of humor and a penchant for practical jokes.<ref name="UA" /> They had traditional proverbs, such as the one saying that the path to success tended to be long.<ref name="J5" /> However, if betrayed or angered, Kushibans could be cruel or vindictive, and they had little patience for bullies, simpletons and misanthropes.<ref name="UA" />

Only rarely a Kushiban showed interest in leaving his or her native Kushibah; the only Kushibans traveling through the galaxy were almost exclusively scholars, scouts and members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /> While some Kushibans joined the Jedi Order, the Kushiban community found the idea of a Kushiban Jedi, or its need, to be laughable.<ref name="J4" /> Kushibans rarely spoke Galactic Basic Standard language,<ref name="UA" /> instead learning only their own [[Kushiban (language)|Kushiban]].<ref name="PJ" /><ref name="NJ" /><ref name="UA" /> Kushibans outside the planet gathered [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and the planet itself had a "pool of credits" to be used by the species.<ref name="EA" />

Due to the fur, short height and long ears of the Kushibans,<ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /> other people tended to take them for pets or vermin.<ref name="NJ" /><ref name="PJ" /><ref name="UA" /><ref name="EA" /> By looking helpless and cute,<ref name="NJ" /> a Kushiban could trick even a Jedi community so that he could be considered the pet of a person instead of an intelligent person.<ref name="J5" />

Kushibans had one-word names, with examples including Asani, Cerlyk, Etyk, Mabbik, Pritsi, Sibble, Tieko, Widdimur and Mirax,<ref name="UA" /> which was also a common name for female [[Human/Legends|Humans]]<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> as in the case of Human [[Mirax Terrik Horn]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].<ref name="XWRS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>

==History==
The planet Kushibah joined the greater galactic community at some point between [[3000 BBY|3000]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], during the times of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="ATL">''[[Junior Jedi Knights: Anakin's Quest]]'' identified [[Kushibah]] as the Kushiban home planet. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion]]'' gave the coordinates of Kushibah. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' then reported events taking place in that coordinates.</ref> identified [[Kushibah]] as the Kushiban home planet. It was uncommon for a Jedi Master to visit Kushiban villages looking for a prospective student; this happened at least once in the last millennia of the Republic, with Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] visiting, and the event made Ikrit feel honored. Yoda took the Kushiban Ikrit as his apprentice, even though the Kushibans laughed and joked at the idea. Ikrit later returned to Kushibah as a Jedi student and saved his community from the Xinkras.<ref name="J4" />

In the years before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Kushiban species was known in the outer galaxy: the Jedi called [[Rune (Jedi)|Rune]], during an interview hosted by [[Yara Grugara]], insulted his college [[Nute (Jedi)|Nute]] calling him a "Kushiban-loving freak."<ref name="WZ">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/livingforce/lf20051110InAllThings|text=In All Things Balance}}</ref> At the same time, in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the species was so rarely seen that the Kushiban K'Mi was being exhibited in an illegal zoo with other exotic species.<ref name="AM" /> Before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], however, Ikrit was featured among the Jedi distributed by [[Biscuit Baron]] featuring several Jedi Masters and other heroes of the Republic.<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|business/13328_2.html|Biscuit Baron to Celebrate "Heroes of the Republic"}}</ref>

During the Clone Wars, Kushibah was in the space controlled by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="ATL" />

Much later, during the struggle against the [[Diversity Alliance]] ([[23 ABY/Legends|23]]-[[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]]), several Kushibans were killed along with [[Bith/Legends|Bith]], [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] and [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaughts]]. [[Raabakyysh]] the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] thought that Kushibans were people same as Humans, and that they should be granted the right to live.<ref name="Y1">''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Emperor's Plague]]''</ref>

===Kushibans in the galaxy===
Ikrit was a Kushiban Jedi Master who gained notoriety during the times of the Galactic Republic. He hibernated for four centuries in the [[Palace of the Woolamander]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], guarding the [[Golden Globe]] while he waited for more prepared Jedi who could break the Globe and free the poor souls trapped inside. As he was sleeping, he avoided the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] and, in the times of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Ikrit awoke and worked along with Junior Jedi Knights [[Anakin Solo]] and Tahiri Veila<ref name="J1" /> to succeed at the Golden Globe quest.<ref name="J3" /> Ikrit later taught Solo and Veila as their Master and worked with them and with other Jedi against threats such as [[Orloc]],<ref name="J4" /><ref name="J5" /><ref name="J6" /> up until the moment where Ikrit heroically sacrificed his life during the war against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="EV3" />

The Kushiban K'Mi was captured, enslaved and regularly beaten by the [[Chevin/Legends|Chevin]] criminal [[Phylus Mon]]. Mon surgically inserted a bomb near K'Mi's heart so he could not escape, and exhibited K'Mi in an illegal zoo in Mon's starship, ''[[Animiasma]]''. K'Mi, unhappy with his fate, was ready to work along any other person to escape.<ref name="AM" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Kushibans first appeared in the novel ''[[Junior Jedi Knights: The Golden Globe]]'', by [[Nancy Richardson Fischer|Nancy Richardson]]. ''The Golden Globe'' was the first novel of six in the ''[[Star Wars: Junior Jedi Knights]]'' series, all of which featured Ikrit the Kushiban as an important character.

On the "Dramatis Personae" section of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'', Kushiban are incorrectly named as an unidentified species.
